title: Rorr'hn of Drgi

'''Rorr'hn of Drgi''',<ref name="Galaxy7" /> who shared their name with the [[Drgi system/Legends|Drgi system]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Tokamac sector/Legends|Tokamac sector]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> served as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]<ref name="Galaxy7" /> at some point after [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]<ref name="Timeframe">{{GalaxyCite|7|Design An Alien}} establishes that Rorr'hn of Drgi [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] and depicts them wearing a [[Rebel flight suit|flight suit]] with an [[Alliance Starbird|insignia]] on it that was used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] ({{CSWECite|III|85|Rebel Alliance}}) and its successor organizations (''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', {{CSWECite|II|375|New Republic}}, {{CSWECite|I|308|Galactic Alliance}}). ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' establishes that the Alliance acquired the resources necessary to start the production of X-wing [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].</ref> and [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]],<ref name="Galaxy7" /> a type of [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> and its successor organizations.<ref name="EGTW" /><ref name="Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref>

Rorr'hn of Drgi was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] being. They had dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] and tan skin and [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and legs with long [[Claw/Legends|claws]]. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] also possessed a large beak-like protrusion under their gray [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that constituted the bulk of their face, in addition to twin horns atop their head. Rorr'hn of Drgi wore a yellow-colored [[Rebel flight suit|flight suit]] with an [[Alliance Starbird]] on it. They wielded a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] with a yellow blade and piloted an X-wing starfighter.<ref name="Galaxy7" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rorr'hn of Drgi was created by [[Tom Whalen]] through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition run in the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]''.<ref name="Galaxy7" /> The competition, launched in the magazine's [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|third issue]]<ref name="Galaxy3">{{GalaxyCite|3|Design An Alien|Design An Alien Contest}}</ref> in [[April]] [[1995]],<ref name="DateGalaxy3">{{GalaxyCite|2|Rebel Report}}</ref> called for readers to create completely new alien [[Species/Legends|species]] inspired by the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. Whalen was twenty-one years old when they submitted their entry on Rorr'hn of Drgi, which was later published in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seventh issue]]<ref name="Galaxy7" /> in April [[1996]],<ref name="DateGalaxy7">{{GalaxyCite|6|Around the Galaxy}}</ref> under the "honorable mentions" section of the "16 and up" subdivision.<ref name="Galaxy7" /> [[Lucasfilm]] employee [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], later stated that published material from the "Design An Alien" competition was considered [[canon]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote='''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon?''' Yes, though any text discrepencies [sic] with other sources would [sic] likely to superceded [sic] by the other source.}}</ref>
